info.title: Your Bourse Trading Platform REST API
info.description:
This document serves as high-level specifications for [Your Bourse](https://yourbourse.com)'s trading platform REST APIs.

All requests should contain the `X-YB-API-Key` header with the public API token.

All field names and values are **case-sensitive**, unless noted otherwise.

# HMAC authentication

All `POST`, `PUT` and `DELETE` requests must contain HMAC signature. For [HMAC authentication](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HMAC) a pair of private and public API tokens is used.

There are two methods to generate HMAC signature: 1) with nonce, or 2) with timestamp.

**1) Nonce + HMAC signature:**

1. Add `X-YB-Nonce` header with nonce value. Please note that its value should be higher than any nonce sent in the previous requests.

2. Build the string to sign by concatenating the following lines exactly in the order shown, separated by a single new line character (`\n`). The string has no trailing newline. If the request has no body (e.g., `DELETE` requests), the value after `Content=` is an empty string but the `Content=` line and the `\n` separator are still required.

   *Example with non-empty body:*
   ```
   Content={ "symbol": "EURUSD", "quantity": 1.23 }
   Nonce=1464264688310
   ```

   *Example with empty body (e.g., `DELETE` requests):*
   ```
   Content=
   Nonce=1464264688310
   ```

3. Calculate the HMAC hash by applying SHA2-256 algorithm.

4. Add the `X-YB-Sign` header to store HMAC hash.

**2) Timestamp + HMAC signature:**

1. Add `X-YB-Timestamp` header with timestamp value (microseconds since Unix epoch).

2. Build the string to sign by concatenating the following lines exactly in the order shown, separated by a single new line character (`\n`). The string has no trailing newline. If the request has no body (e.g., `DELETE` requests), the value after `Content=` is an empty string but the `Content=` line and the `\n` separator are still required.

   *Example with non-empty body:*
   ```
   Content={ "symbol": "EURUSD", "quantity": 1.23 }
   Timestamp=1738066591010786
   ```

   *Example with empty body (e.g., `DELETE` requests):*
   ```
   Content=
   Timestamp=1738066591010786
   ```

3. Calculate the HMAC hash by applying SHA2-256 algorithm.

4. Add the `X-YB-Sign` header to store HMAC hash.

**Example: signing a `DELETE /order/{orderId}` request**

Below is a complete `curl` example for cancelling an order. Because the request body is empty, the value of `Content` in the string to sign is an empty string, but the `Content=` line and the `\n` separator before `Nonce=` (or `Timestamp=`) must still be present. The HMAC hash is base64url-encoded (RFC 4648 §5) without `=` padding.

```bash
API_KEY="vJJErCZDZBD07z1p4Ed91t0qob6bAL"
SECRET="frieUMo6AdidOMp7XQVSfGRTRyF5hktoRX5Pk0ijujatIHBbNvXQSO4kUyirSHF"
ORDER_ID=12345
NONCE=1464264688310

# String to sign — literally `Content=\nNonce=<nonce>` with no trailing newline.
STRING_TO_SIGN=$(printf 'Content=\nNonce=%s' "$NONCE")

# HMAC-SHA256, then base64url without `=` padding.
SIGN=$(printf '%s' "$STRING_TO_SIGN" \
  | openssl dgst -sha256 -hmac "$SECRET" -binary \
  | openssl base64 -A | tr '+/' '-_' | tr -d '=')

curl -X DELETE "https://<host>:<port>/api/v1/order/$ORDER_ID" \
  -H "X-YB-API-Key: $API_KEY" \
  -H "X-YB-Nonce: $NONCE" \
  -H "X-YB-Sign: $SIGN"
```

To sign with a timestamp instead, use a microsecond timestamp in place of the nonce, write `Timestamp=` instead of `Nonce=` in the string to sign, and send it in the `X-YB-Timestamp` header instead of `X-YB-Nonce`.

# Conditional GETs

If a GET request to a resource does not have the `If-None-Match` header, server always returns resource version in the `ETag` header.

If a GET request to a resource has the `If-None-Match` header with the last known resource version but the resource has been updated since, server responds with a new resource representation and includes the new `ETag` header in the response.

If the value of `If-None-Match` header of a GET request matches the current resource version on the server, the server responds with the `304 Not Modified` status code and empty body. The server includes the `ETag` header to the response.

# Conditional POSTs, PUTs and DELETEs

All `POST`, `PUT` and `DELETE` requests must contain the `If-Match` header with the last known resource version.

If the value of `If-Match` header of the request matches the current resource version on the server, the server proceeds with the request. Otherwise, the server responds with the `412 Precondition Failed` status. Client is expected to retrieve the latest resource version and retry the request.

If the `If-Match` header is not present in the request, status code of `412 Precondition Failed` is returned along with the error details.

# HTTP Return Codes
| Status code | Error |
| ----- | ----- |
| `304 Not Modified` | The requested resource has not been modified since the last time it was accessed. |
| `400 Bad Request` | Invalid request (e.g., malformed request syntax). |
| `401 Unauthorized` | 'X-YB-API-Key' header is missing or invalid. |
| `403 Forbidden` | IP has been auto-banned for continuing to send requests after receiving `429 Too Many Requests` codes. |
| `404 Not Found` | Either the resource does not exist or client is not permitted to access it. |
| `412 Precondition Failed` | Conditional request failed. |
| `413 Request Entity Too Large` | Request size is greater than a pre-defined threshold (security measure to avoid DDoS attacks). |
| `415 Unsupported Media Type` | Body is not in the `application/json` format. |
| `429 Too Many Requests` | The client has sent too many requests in a given amount of time and broke a request rate limit. |
| `500 Internal Server Error` | An unexpected error occurred on server; the issue is on Your Bourse's side. It is important to **NOT** treat this as a failure operation; the execution status is **UNKNOWN** and could have been a success. |

# Rate limits

The rate limits are based on the IP addresses, not the API keys.

Each route has a `weight` which determines for the number of requests each endpoint counts for. Heavier endpoints and endpoints that do operations on multiple symbols will have a heavier `weight`.

When a `429 Too Many Requests` is received, it's your obligation as an API to back off and not spam the API. Repeatedly violating rate limits and/or failing to back off after receiving 429s will result in an automated IP ban (HTTP status `403 Forbidden`). IP bans are tracked and scale in duration for repeat offenders.

A `Retry-After` header is sent with a `403 Forbidden` or `429 Too Many Requests` responses and will give the number of seconds required to wait, in the case of a 429, to prevent a ban, or, in the case of a 403, until the ban is over.

Every response might contain `X-YB-USED-WEIGHT-(intervalNum)(intervalLetter)` headers with the used weight for the IP address for all request rate limiters defined.

`intervalLetter` could be `S` (second), `M` (minute), `H` (hour) or `D` (day). `intervalNum` describes the amount of the interval. For example, `intervalNum` 10 with `intervalLetter` S means "Every 10 seconds".

# Unfilled order count limits

Every successful order response might contain a `X-YB-ORDER-COUNT-(intervalNum)(intervalLetter)` header indicating how many orders you have placed for that interval. Rejected/unsuccessful orders are not guaranteed to have `X-YB-ORDER-COUNT-(intervalNum)(intervalLetter)` headers in the response.

The rate limit applies to the number of new, unfilled orders placed within a time interval. That is, orders which are partially or fully filled do not count against the rate limit.

Unfilled order count is tracked by trading account and is shared across all IP addresses, all API keys, and all APIs.

If you have exceeded this, you will receive a `429 Too Many Requests` error without the `Retry-After` header.

# HTTP keep-alive

It is strongly recommended to enable `Keep-Alive` and reuse HTTP connections to send and receive multiple HTTP requests/responses, as opposed to opening a new connection for every single request/response pair.

broker.description: Optional broker/company name for validation. If provided, must match the company name associated with the user's group. Maximum length is 63 characters.

get.description:
Get rate limits and unfilled order count limits.

`Weight` 5.

Returns configuration for all available symbols.

`Weight` 20.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sorting by symbol name in ascending (`asc`) or descending (`desc`) direction. Ignored if `nextToken` is present.

post.description:
Get trading account information.

Consider using WebSocket API instead, as it can decrease the workload on the servers.

`Weight` 20.

Get working and inactive orders.

Consider using WebSocket API instead, as it can decrease the workload on the servers.

`Weight` 20.

post.summary: Get single open order by order id
post.description:
Get working or inactive order.

Consider using WebSocket API instead, as it can decrease the workload on the servers.

`Weight` 4.

Get order history for an account. Returned orders will have a final status (`Rejected`,
`Filled`, `Cancelled` and `Expired`).

`Weight` 20.

Returns single order in a final state (Rejected, Filled, Cancelled and Expired). Available history depths is limited due to performance reasons.

`Weight` 20.

put.summary: Modify stop loss and/or take profit of order
put.description:
Modify stop loss and/or take profit of order.

`Weight` 1.

put.summary: Modify stop loss and/or take profit of position
put.description:
Modify stop loss and/or take profit of position.

`Weight` 1.

post.description:
Get positions for an account.

Consider using WebSocket API instead, as it can decrease the workload on the servers.

`Weight` 20.

get.description:
Get current depth of market for the instrument.
`Weight` 25.

parameters.name: depth
parameters.description: Max number of price bands in response.

get.description:
Get the best bid and ask of the instrument.

`Weight` 2.

post.description:
Get cash/asset transfers history for an account.

`Weight` 20.

Get conversion rate for a single currency pair.

`Weight` 2.

Get conversion rates for multiple currency pairs.

Maximum 100 items per request.

`Weight` 5.

get.description:
Returns current time and server version. 

`Weight` 1.

Nonce.name: X-YB-Nonce
Nonce.description: Nonce (must be larger than previous nonces). Required if HMAC signature is generated using nonce.

Timestamp.name: X-YB-Timestamp
Timestamp.description: Timestamp (microseconds since Unix epoch). Required if HMAC signature is generated using timestamp.

NextToken.name: X-YB-NEXT-TOKEN
NextToken.description: The `nextToken` returned by a previous call, which can be used to retrieve the next set of results.

HistoryFrom.name: from
HistoryFrom.description: Timestamp (microseconds since the Unix epoch) of the leftmost required bar, including `from`.

HistoryTo.name: to
HistoryTo.description: Timestamp (microseconds since the Unix epoch) of the rightmost required bar, including `to`. It can be in the future. In this case, the rightmost required bar is the latest available bar.

MaxResults.name: maxResults
MaxResults.description: The maximum number of results returned by the call. Ignored if `nextToken` is present.

ApiErrorCode.title: ApiErrorCode
ApiErrorCode.description:
Numeric error code, returned as a string. The following table lists all possible values:
| Code | Description                          | |------|--------------------------------------| | "0"  | Successful operation                 | | "1"  | Not found                            | | "2"  | Server is not ready                  | | "3"  | Invalid parameter                    | | "4"  | Invalid target                       | | "5"  | Invalid 'NextToken'                  | | "6"  | Invalid header                       | | "7"  | Invalid version                      | | "8"  | Internal error                       | | "9"  | Dependencies exist                   | | "10" | Already exists                       | | "11" | Invalid payload                      | | "12" | Not allowed                          | | "13" | Operation not permitted              | | "14" | Business logic error                 | | "15" | Invalid Id                           | | "16" | Data changed                         | | "17" | Identifier cannot be reused          | | "100"| No market depth                      | | "101"| Invalid market depth                 |

ProblemDetails.title: A simplified representation of RFC 9457 "Problem Details for HTTP APIs"

title.description: A short, human-readable localised summary of the problem type.

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.

account.description: Trading account ID associated with this token.

token.description:
API token acts as a session ID that the application uses for making
requests. This token should be protected as if it were user
credentials.

signingToken.description:
Signing token used as the secret key when preparing HMAC signatures
for API requests. This token should be protected as if it were user
credentials.

expiration.description:
The time when the token is expired is represented as the number of
microseconds since the Unix epoch (00:00:00 UTC on 1 January 1970).

id.description: Unique Account Summary Row item identifier.

title.description: Localised Account Summary Row item title.

tooltip.description: Tooltip that is shown on mouse hover.

alignment.description: The cell value alignment. Default value is `left`.

isCapitalize.description: The first character of each word in the sentence will be capitalized. The rest of the symbols appearance does not change.

hasDatePicker.description: Display date control in Order Ticket for this duration type.

hasTimePicker.description: Display time control in Order Ticket for this duration type.

default.description: Default duration. Only one duration object in the durations array can have a `true` value for this field. The default duration will be used when the user places orders in the silent mode and it will be the selected one when the user opens the Order dialog for the first time.

supportedOrderTypes.description: An optional array of order types to which the duration will be applied.

PullingInterval.title: PullingInterval
PullingInterval.description:
Time intervals in milliseconds to pull data from the server.
Please note: if the pulling interval is set above the maximum value then the maximum value will be used explicitly.

quotes.description: Time interval in milliseconds to request quote and level 2 depth updates.

orders.description: Time interval in milliseconds to request orders.

positions.description: Time interval in milliseconds to request positions.

accountManager.description: Time interval in milliseconds to update Account manager tables.

balances.description: Time interval in milliseconds to request balances.

PositionCustomFields.title: PositionCustomFields
PositionCustomFields.description: Position columns to be displayed in the positions table of the Account manager.

OrderCustomFields.title: OrderCustomFields
OrderCustomFields.description: Order columns to be displayed in the orders table of the Account manager.

OrderHistoryCustomFields.title: OrderHistoryCustomFields
OrderHistoryCustomFields.description: History columns to be displayed in the history table of the Account manager.

l.description: Quantity of a stock, commodity, financial instrument, or units of base currency in 1 lot.

tv.description: Tick value (monetary value of 1 tick price change).

tz.description: Tick size (the lowest allowed increment of a price change).

min.description: Minimum order size (in lots, up to 8 decimal places).

max.description: Maximum order size (in lots, up to 8 decimal places).

i.description: Order size increment (in lots, up to 8 decimal places).

pos.description: Maximum total position size (in lots, up to 8 decimal places). 0 - no limit.

bdp.description: Pricing decimal precision for base currency.

pdp.description: Pricing decimal precision for profit currency.

mdp.description: Pricing decimal precision for margin currency.

M.description: true - market orders are allowed, false - market orders are prohibited.

L.description: true - limit orders are allowed, false - limit orders are prohibited.

S.description: true - stop orders are allowed, false - stop orders are prohibited.

SLi.description: true - stop limit orders are allowed, false - stop limit orders are prohibited.

SLo.description: true - stop loss orders are allowed, false - stop loss orders are prohibited.

TP.description: true - take profit orders are allowed, false - take profit orders are prohibited.

CB.description: true - close by/close all orders are allowed, false - close by/close all orders are prohibited.

fok.description: true - 'Fill Or Kill' orders are allowed, false - 'Fill Or Kill' orders are prohibited.

ioc.description: true - 'Immediate or Cancel' orders are allowed, false - 'Immediate or Cancel' orders are prohibited.

gtc.description: true - 'Good till Cancelled' orders are allowed, false - 'Good till Cancelled' orders are prohibited.

gtd.description: true - 'Good till Date' orders are allowed, false - 'Good till Date' orders are prohibited.

day.description: true - day orders are allowed, false - day orders are prohibited.

ms.description: true - 'Valid For Milliseconds' orders are allowed, false - 'Valid For Milliseconds' orders are prohibited.

swYd.description: Number of days in year for swap calculation (360 or 365).

swSt.description: Swap intraday starting time in HH:MM:SS format. Only present when `swI` is not `EndOfDay`.

swI.description: Swap charge interval. When set to `EndOfDay`, swap is charged once at the end of the trading day and `swSt` field is not included. When set to an intraday value, swap is charged at regular intervals starting from the time specified in `swSt`.

pct.description: Margin percentage multiplier applied to every formula (100 = no change).

hm.description: Hedged margin fraction charged for the hedged portion (0.0–1.0).

im.description: Per-lot initial margin. Used in Futures mode only.

mm.description: Per-lot maintenance margin. Used in Futures mode only.

lmr.description: Liquidity margin rate. Asset contribution to equity in Currency/Collateral mode.

mrIB.description: Initial margin rate for buy market orders and open buy positions.

mrIS.description: Initial margin rate for sell market orders and open sell positions.

mrIBl.description: Initial margin rate for pending buy limit orders.

mrISl.description: Initial margin rate for pending sell limit orders.

mrIBt.description: Initial margin rate for pending buy stop orders.

mrISt.description: Initial margin rate for pending sell stop orders.

mrIBtl.description: Initial margin rate for pending buy stop-limit orders.

mrIStl.description: Initial margin rate for pending sell stop-limit orders.

mrMB.description: Maintenance margin rate for open buy positions.

mrMS.description: Maintenance margin rate for open sell positions.

mrMBl.description: Maintenance margin rate for pending buy limit orders.

mrMSl.description: Maintenance margin rate for pending sell limit orders.

mrMBt.description: Maintenance margin rate for pending buy stop orders.

mrMSt.description: Maintenance margin rate for pending sell stop orders.

mrMBtl.description: Maintenance margin rate for pending buy stop-limit orders.

mrMStl.description: Maintenance margin rate for pending sell stop-limit orders.

mll.description: Use the larger leg for hedged margin calculation.

reod.description: Recalculate margin rates at end of day.

mchk.description: Check margin before executing SL/TP orders.

nextToken.description: A token indicating there are more results available. If there are no more results, no token is included in the response. The value of `nextToken` is a unique pagination token for each page. To retrieve the next page of results, make the call again using the returned token. Keep all other arguments unchanged. Using an expired pagination token results in an `412 Precondition Failed` error.

A token indicating there are more results available. If there are no more results, no token is included in the response. The value of `nextToken` is a unique pagination token for each page. To retrieve the next page of results, make the call again using the returned token. Keep all other arguments unchanged. Using an expired pagination token results in an `412 Precondition Failed` error.

OrderStatus.description:
String constants to describe an order status.

Status  | Description
----------|-------------
Inactive  | Stop Loss or Take Profit order is created but waiting for a base order to be filled.
Working   | Order is created and active but not executed yet.
PartiallyFilled    | Order is partially filled and still active.
Filled    | Order is fully filled.
Cancelled  | Order has been cancelled.
Rejected  | Order has been rejected.
Expired  | Order has expired.

ap.description: Average price of order fills (if any).

lp.description: Limit price for limit or stop limit orders only.

sp.description: Stop price for stop or stop limit orders only.

poi.description: Identifier of a parent order. Should be set only for bracket orders.

ppi.description: Identifier of a parent position. Should be set only for bracket orders.

tt.description: If `tif` is `gtd` - microseconds since the Unix epoch; if `tif` is `ms` - milliseconds since order placement.

C.description: Date/time when the order was created (microseconds since the Unix epoch).

M.description: Date/time when the order was last modified (microseconds since the Unix epoch).

slid.description: Stop Loss order unique identifier (if SL is set)

tpid.description: Take Profit order unique identifier (if TP is set)

OrderInformationalMessage.title: OrderInformationalMessage
OrderInformationalMessage.description: Informational message description, that may be displayed for the order in the client terminal.

m.example: This order has been rejected due to the closed market

a.description: Market value of collateral adjusted by liquidity margin rate.

lp.description: Limit price for limit or stop limit order.

sp.description: Stop price for stop or stop limit order.

tt.description: If `tif` is `GTD` - microseconds since the Unix epoch; if `tif` is `Ms` - milliseconds since order placement.

q.description: New remaining quantity in lots. The new total order volume will be equal to the new remaining quantity plus the already filled quantity. Cannot be modified for stop loss or take profit orders attached to a parent order or position.

lp.description: Limit price for limit or stop limit order.

sp.description: Stop price for stop or stop limit order.

ModifyPositionSLTP.title: Modify position's stop loss and/or take profit

sl.description: Stop loss price. If not set, stop loss order is cancelled.

tp.description: Take profit price. If not set, take profit order is cancelled.

ModifyOrderSLTP.title: Modify order's stop loss and/or take profit

sl.description: Stop loss price. If not set, stop loss order is cancelled.

tp.description: Take profit price. If not set, take profit order is cancelled.

p.description: Volume-weighted average price (VWAP) of position trades.

C.description: Date/time when the position was created (microseconds since the Unix epoch).

M.description: Date/time when the position was last modified (microseconds since the Unix epoch).

Ci.description: Unique identifier of the order which created the position.

Mi.description: Unique identifier of the order which last modified the position.

pC.description: Profit-to-account-currency conversion rate at the time of the last update.

mC.description: Margin-to-account-currency conversion rate at the time of the last update.

nextToken.description: A token indicating there are more results available. If there are no more results, no token is included in the response. The value of `nextToken` is a unique pagination token for each page. To retrieve the next page of results, make the call again using the returned token. Keep all other arguments unchanged. Using an expired pagination token results in an `412 Precondition Failed` error.

A token indicating there are more results available. If there are no more results, no token is included in the response. The value of `nextToken` is a unique pagination token for each page. To retrieve the next page of results, make the call again using the returned token. Keep all other arguments unchanged. Using an expired pagination token results in an `412 Precondition Failed` error.

t.description: Execution time (microseconds since the Unix epoch).

oi.description: Unique identifier of the order that has been filled.

pi.description: Unique identifier of the position that has been opened, modified or closed.

TimeInForce.description: Time in Force. Only `FOK` and `IOC` are valid values for `CloseBy` and `Market` orders.

nextToken.description: A token indicating there are more results available. If there are no more results, no token is included in the response. The value of `nextToken` is a unique pagination token for each page. To retrieve the next page of results, make the call again using the returned token. Keep all other arguments unchanged. Using an expired pagination token results in an `412 Precondition Failed` error.

id.description: Transaction ID. Should be 0 for a new cash transfer.

t.description: Transaction date/time (microseconds since the Unix epoch).

nextToken.description: A token indicating there are more results available. If there are no more results, no token is included in the response. The value of `nextToken` is a unique pagination token for each page. To retrieve the next page of results, make the call again using the returned token. Keep all other arguments unchanged. Using an expired pagination token results in an `412 Precondition Failed` error.

av.description: The balance available to the user.

r.description: Liquidity margin rate. For example, if it is set to 0.5, only 50% of market value can be used for collateral purposes.

t.description: Bar start time (microseconds since the Unix epoch).

nextToken.description: A token indicating there are more results available. If there are no more results, no token is included in the response. The value of `nextToken` is a unique pagination token for each page. To retrieve the next page of results, make the call again using the returned token. Keep all other arguments unchanged. Using an expired pagination token results in an `412 Precondition Failed` error.

Book.title: Book
Book.description: Depth of market for an instrument.

a.description: Array of arrays with two numeric elements - price and volume. Sorted by `price` in ascending order.

b.description: Array of arrays with two numeric elements - price and volume. Sorted by `price` in descending order.

ah.description: Ask high - highest ask price for the day.

al.description: Ask low - lowest ask price for the day.

bh.description: Bid high - highest bid price for the day.

bl.description: Bid low - lowest bid price for the day.

bc.description: Bid close - last bid price of the previous trading day.

ac.description: Ask close - last ask price of the previous trading day.

BookItem.title: BookItem
BookItem.description: Array with two numeric elements - price and volume.

Quote.title: Quote
Quote.description: Top of the book prices for an instrument.

bh.description: Bid high - highest bid price for the day.

bl.description: Bid low - lowest bid price for the day.

ah.description: Ask high - highest ask price for the day.

al.description: Ask low - lowest ask price for the day.

bc.description: Bid close - last bid price of the previous trading day.

ac.description: Ask close - last ask price of the previous trading day.

i.description: Interval: `S` - second, `M` - minute, `H` - hour, and `D` - day.

Limits.title: Limits
Limits.description: Rate limits and unfilled order count limits.

Now.title: Health check
Now.description: Health check (returns server version, current time, and server timezone).

SortOrder.description: Sorting in ascending (`asc`) or descending (`desc`) direction

HistoryFrom.title: Start of the period
HistoryFrom.description: Timestamp (microseconds since the Unix epoch) of the start of the period.

HistoryTo.title: End of the period
HistoryTo.description: Timestamp (microseconds since the Unix epoch) of the end of the period. It can be in the future.

maxResults.description: The maximum number of results returned by the call. If more than 100 results is requested, the server will return 100 items.
The maximum number of results returned by the call. If more than 100 results is requested, the server will return 100 items.
The maximum number of results returned by the call. If more than 100 results is requested, the server will return 100 items.
The maximum number of results returned by the call. If more than 100 results is requested, the server will return 100 items.
The maximum number of results returned by the call. If more than 100 results is requested, the server will return 100 items.
The expected number of results. Server can return more bars.

SingleConversionRateRequest.title: Single conversion rate request
SingleConversionRateRequest.description: Request for a single conversion rate

ConversionRatesBatchRequest.title: Conversion rates batch request
ConversionRatesBatchRequest.description: Array of currency pairs for conversion rates (maximum 100 items)

rate.description: Conversion rate (how many units of target currency per 1 unit of source currency)

ConversionRateBatchResult.title: Conversion rate batch result
ConversionRateBatchResult.description: Result for a single currency pair in batch request

r.description: Conversion rate. Present only on success.

d.description: Human-readable details in case of an error

c.description: Error code in case of an error

ConversionRatesBatchResponse.title: Conversion rates batch response
ConversionRatesBatchResponse.description: Array of conversion rate results corresponding to input pairs
